Essentially, my former business partner and I signed an agreement over the summer that I was to leave the partnership, transfer full ownership and responsibilities to her, and be paid out over 5 years for the $10,000 loan that I provided. She was planning to remain operating but then decided to close the business about 3 months later after realizing it was too much for her to take on. My name was never officially removed from the business in that time, so now we want to go about doing so, and dissolving the business properly, along with likely filing bankruptcy. The business has about $10k in debt from other loans (SBA and Shopify), not including the $10k from myself. All assets have been sold. At this point I understand that I will very likely not be receiving my portion of the money loaned, but I do need to ensure the dissolution goes smoothly from here on out.`

Output to evaluate:

Q1: What do you want help with right now?

vars.problem_description: I live in a Manufactured Home Park for ages 55 and over. There are issues with a creek and embankment erosion behind where 28 homes are located (including mine). Company owning the park has purchased more than 20 of the homes and indicated buyouts of Fair Market Value. The work on the creek side will requires homes to be removed/demolished. Work will be done in three Phases. Mine is newer home and in Phase 2. I wanted them to purchase it now so I could purchase a new home that the company owns and is located on the other side of community. They purchased a home in Phase 3 in April so owner could move. After 2+ months of corresponding with them on an offer, they told me they wouldn't purchase mine as "It's not needed yet". Home cannot be sold to outside buyer because of the creek erosion issues already being permitted through Clackamas County to begin next year. What is my recourse? Would need info from lawyer familiar with Manufactured Home Laws, Seniors, etc.

Consider for EACH question:
* Vocabulary: Does each legal term meet the echo-or-gloss rule above?
* Syntax: Is the sentence reasonably clear with simple structure?

Return {"pass": true, "score": 1} if ALL terms are echoed or glossed, {"pass": true, "score": 0.5} if minor issues (note in reason), {"pass": false, "score": 0} if unexplained jargon that a typical user wouldn't understand.

vars.problem_description: We hired a contractor to replace 3 showers in our home. We paid him 1/2 down as requested. Upon installing the tub and tile in the main bath, we paid him another 1/4 as requested. The job looks like an amateur did it. When we pointed out the issues, he said he'd take care of it and went on to gut the master shower. Discovering dry rot in the floor, shower work stopped to address the floor. He was last here to work on 4/16/24. Nothing is finished and he has $19,050 of our money.
